analysis.summary: The technician connected to the client's computer using GoToAssist, navigated to a specific directory on a local drive, deleted an old script file, and presumably transferred a new script file. After some hesitation, the technician launched an application named 'Agent Launch' and then ended the remote session.
actions_taken: Established a remote support connection via GoToAssist.
Navigated the file system to the 'Optimization\opti\O005\utils' directory on the 'Data (F:)' drive.
Launched the 'Agent Launch' application (part of the Optimization software).
Clicked 'OK' on the 'Agent Launch' application window.
issues_observed: The technician repeatedly right-clicked on 'new_script.sh' without taking action after deleting the old script, suggesting momentary uncertainty.
issues_resolved: An old script file ('new_script_old.sh') was replaced with a new one ('new_script.sh'), which likely resolved an issue or provided an update related to the 'Optimization' software.
notable_events: The use of a complex URL (`link.edgepilot.com/v/id8d40031/f/uAjQpAyBEITw=/?u=https://flashsupport.gotoassist.com/678201007`) to initiate the remote session.
The technician appeared uncertain for a period after deleting the old script, repeatedly right-clicking the new script without action.

tech_performance.efficiency_notes: The technician efficiently initiated the remote session and navigated to the target directory. However, there was a noticeable period of hesitation/uncertainty (approx. 18 seconds) after deleting the old script, where the technician repeatedly right-clicked the new script without selecting an action, and a subsequent period of low activity before launching the application.
coaching_flags: Review the period of indecision (1:26 - 1:44) and the subsequent low activity (1:44 - 2:06) to ensure clarity on next steps during file replacement tasks.
